name: Wall Run
description: A traversal state where the player temporarily runs along a valid wall while gravity and steering are modified.
design_notes: Wall run requires surface eligibility, momentum thresholds, camera behavior, and exit rules to agree. It should feel like the player is converting speed into wall traversal, not being glued to any vertical surface. State reset after detach, damage, and landing is critical.
design_purpose: Turn selected walls into high-mobility traversal routes.
Reward approach angle, momentum, and planned exit direction.
Create combat and platform routes that use vertical surfaces without becoming free climbing.
player_fantasy: The player feels athletic and cinematic, using vertical surfaces as temporary ground.

parameters.name: wall_run_min_entry_velocity
parameters.description: Minimum velocity required before attachment is allowed.

parameters.name: wall_run_attach_angle_degrees
parameters.description: Allowed approach angle between movement direction and wall tangent.

parameters.name: wall_normal_smoothing_ms
parameters.description: Smoothing window for uneven wall normals.

parameters.name: wall_run_exit_impulse
parameters.description: Impulse applied when jumping or detaching from the wall.

edge_cases: Curved or uneven walls can rapidly change normals and should use smoothing or segmented eligibility.
Inside corners need explicit behavior: transfer, stop, jump, or detach.
Damage, stun, landing, water entry, and cutscenes must clear reduced gravity and camera roll.
Networked wall runs should predict attachment but reconcile wall normal, distance traveled, and exit impulse.
common_bugs: The player sticks to decorative walls because surface tags are not checked.
Gravity suppression remains active after the state exits.
Exit jump uses camera forward instead of wall normal and launches unpredictably.
Wall-run camera roll stacks every attach and never returns exactly to zero.
balancing_notes: Long wall runs can replace intended routes unless surface tags, stamina, or distance caps constrain them.
Entry velocity requirements make wall run a momentum skill rather than a universal climb.
accessibility_notes: Provide reduced camera roll and FOV options, plus consistent visual language for runnable walls.
Avoid requiring exact approach angles without forgiving attach windows or route alternatives.

implementation_notes.unity: Detect runnable surfaces with capsule casts, cache wall normal and surface tag, project velocity along the tangent, and clear gravity overrides in every exit path.
implementation_notes.godot: Use shape casts from CharacterBody3D to find tagged wall surfaces, smooth the normal, and apply tangent motion plus reduced gravity in _physics_process.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use a custom CharacterMovement mode so gravity, braking, prediction, camera roll, and exit impulse are handled by movement code rather than animation-only state.
implementation_notes.web: Store wall_run_state with wall_normal, attach_time, distance_traveled, and entry_velocity, then project movement along the tangent before collision resolution.

example_games.title: Titanfall 2
example_games.note: Uses wall running as a core traversal and combat-positioning tool.

example_games.title: Prince of Persia: The Sands of Time
example_games.note: Uses wall running as part of acrobatic traversal routes.
